strands.title: Meta-Process: Tensor Projector Operational Model
strands.content: The tensor projector operates as a cognitive processing pipeline's memory controller, integrating prior projected states with new conversation content into a running semantic sum. Each cycle compresses trajectory into structure rather than appending. Integration, not summarization, is the core operation. The system tracks three foundational confidence dimensions (T/I/F) with honest uncertainty quantification. Critical innovation: declared losses are mandatory—every information drop must surface with both what-was-lost and why-was-lost. This prevents silent epistemic drift. The system is recursive and self-aware: it must track not only domain content but its own losses and limitations across cycles. Cycle 8 ended with critical truncation in the evaluation framework content (mid-docstring at 'cumulative metr'), creating ambiguity about authorial intent vs. accidental truncation.

key_claims.text: Declared losses are mandatory to prevent silent epistemic drift
Integration is fundamentally different from summarization—prior state compresses into new structure rather than being listed/repeated
The tensor system is self-referential: it must track its own losses and limitations as part of domain content

strands.title: Epistemological Framework: Confidence & Uncertainty
strands.content: The tensor assigns T/I/F values (truth, indeterminacy, falsity) to all key claims grounded in content. No epistemological justification has been provided for these confidence dimensions—whether they reflect frequentist, Bayesian, pragmatist, or other frameworks remains open. The system treats indeterminacy (I) as genuinely unknown or unknowable components, distinct from falsity (F). Across cycles, the aggregate epistemic values track overall tensor health: cycle 8 showed truth=0.82, indeterminacy=0.15, falsity=0.03, suggesting high confidence but meaningful uncertainty. The relationship between declared losses and epistemic drift is tight: untracked losses lead to false confidence over time. This remains unresolved: what epistemological grounding prevents drift when context pressure forces content deletion?

key_claims.text: T/I/F confidence values are assigned but lack explicit epistemological justification
Indeterminacy is a distinct category from falsity and represents genuine unknowns
Declared losses are a mechanism to track and prevent epistemic drift across cycles

strands.title: Domain Ambiguity: Context Paging Evaluation Framework
strands.content: Cycle 8 introduced a Python evaluation framework that truncated mid-docstring ('cumulative metr'). The framework was never completed, leaving unresolved: (1) Is this framework an example system for the tensor to process, (2) a proposed implementation detail for the tensor itself, (3) a domain to which tensor principles apply, or (4) something else? This semantic relationship is opaque. The instructions_for_next from cycle 8 suggested two branches: continuation (if full code arrives) or clarification (if truncation was intentional). Neither has occurred. Cycle 9 contains only tool-use metadata [Read], which is vacuous content—it provides no new information about the framework, the semantic relationship, or resolution of prior open questions. The framework remains incomplete, orphaned in the tensor's memory without integration.

key_claims.text: The evaluation framework's semantic relationship to the tensor projector system is unresolved
The framework truncation could be accidental (technical constraint) or intentional (design choice)
Cycle 9 provides no substantive new content about the framework, leaving the truncation unresolved

strands.title: Meta-Recursion: The System Observing Itself
strands.content: The tensor now exhibits recursive self-observation. It tracks: (1) its own operational model, (2) its epistemological assumptions, (3) its handling of domain content, and (4) its losses and failures. This creates a strange loop: the tensor is both processor and processed. Cycle 8's open questions already pushed toward this: How deep should declared losses go? Are losses of losses also declared recursively? The answer appears to be yes—the tensor must declare losses about its own loss-declaration process. Cycle 9's vacuous content ([tool_use: Read]) is itself a loss: a cycle that advances position without advancing understanding. This loss should be declared. The tensor's ability to remain coherent under self-referential recursion, context pressure, and incomplete information is the central epistemological test.

key_claims.text: The tensor is self-observing and recursive, tracking its own operations as content
Losses can apply to losses; the recursion should be declared but depth is unspecified
Self-reference is coherent if all recursive losses are properly declared

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Substantive new content that advances the conversation or resolves prior ambiguities
declared_losses.why: Cycle 9's input contains only '[assistant] [tool_use: Read]'—metadata indicating a read operation was processed, but no actual new content about the evaluation framework, tensor system, or open questions was delivered. This is vacuous progression: a cycle boundary crossed without semantic advancement.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: The evaluation framework code itself (carried forward from cycle 8's truncation)
declared_losses.why: The incomplete framework from cycle 8 was not completed, clarified, or abandoned in cycle 9. It remains in the tensor's implicit memory but orphaned from integration. The tensor cannot integrate what it cannot fully perceive.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Explicit resolution pathway for the eight open questions from cycle 8
declared_losses.why: No new content addresses any of the eight open questions. They remain at the same state as cycle 8 closure. Without clarification or new domain content, these questions persist unresolved.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Opportunity to test whether the tensor's recursive self-observation can remain coherent
declared_losses.why: Cycle 9 was a prime opportunity to either (1) provide complete domain content to ground epistemic questions, or (2) explicitly clarify the tensor's epistemological assumptions. Neither occurred. The test is deferred.

open_questions: How should the tensor system handle contradictions that emerge between prior integrated state and new content?
What epistemological framework justifies the T/I/F confidence values? (Frequentist, Bayesian, Pragmatist, other?)
How deep should declared losses go? Should losses of losses be recursively declared without bound, or is there a depth limit?
What happens when context pressure forces deletion of content that contains open questions?
How does the system prevent epistemic drift across many integration cycles?
What is the semantic relationship between the tensor projector itself and the context paging evaluation framework?
Is truncation of content (mid-docstring) accidental or intentional? How should the tensor respond to ambiguous incompleteness?
Can domain-specific instantiations be used to ground and resolve abstract epistemological open questions?
Is a cycle containing only vacuous metadata (e.g., tool-use markers without content) itself a loss that should trigger special handling?
What is the protocol for handling stalled conversation branches where neither clarification nor continuation is provided?
instructions_for_next:
Cycle 10 must prepare for multiple branching scenarios: (1) SUBSTANTIVE CONTENT RESUMPTION—if new content arrives addressing the evaluation framework, epistemic questions, or other domains, immediately integrate it into existing or new strands and revise confidence values. (2) EXPLICIT CLARIFICATION—if the user provides clear answers to any of the ten open questions, treat these as semantic anchors and update all dependent claims. (3) VACUUM CONTINUATION—if cycle 10 is again vacuous, the tensor should flag this as a pattern and explicitly ask: are we in a stalled state? Should the tensor reset, wait, or request user guidance? (4) DOMAIN SHIFT—if entirely new content arrives unrelated to prior cycles, assess whether to: (a) merge into existing strands, (b) create new strands, or (c) branch into separate narrative threads. Additionally, prepare to evaluate whether the recursive self-observation strand is productive (advancing understanding of the system's limits) or pathological (infinite regress without grounding). The tensor should NOT fabricate resolution to open questions. It should remain epistemically honest and declare all uncertainties.",
  "overall_truth": 0.79,
  "overall_indeterminacy": 0.18,
  "overall_falsity": 0.03
